description.new:
Barulho World returns to Annex, bringing a curated night to Dortmund that moves between club culture and global sonic landscapes.

As a collective, Barulho World stands for a perspective on
electronic music that centers Afro-diasporic influences
and connects local scenes with international currents.

The musical focus lies on
Afro-electronic sounds
in their full range: from Afrohouse and
Afrotech
to
Gqom
,
Baile Funk
and
Kuduro
, extending into genre-crossing hybrid forms. Rather than fixed categories, the emphasis is on music as a connecting force.

A key part of this approach is the integration of live and hybrid elements. Through hardware, performative setups, or voice, the music remains immediate and closely tied to the moment. The dancefloor becomes not only a space for listening, but for experiencing performance itself.

With Frau Beji, Piccell and D[Nowhere]J, different perspectives come together, each embodying this approach and shaping the space both sonically and emotionally.

Admission 25 €

The night moves through early
Detroit
techno
and
Berlin dub techno
with
Fadi Mohem
and
Marcel Dettmann
, delivering a sound rooted in reduction, repetition and forward momentum. Long blends, rolling grooves and subtle shifts in intensity shape the flow of the night from start to finish.

description.new:
A night shaped by
experimental Detroit techno
, genre-bending selections and a steady awareness of the dancefloor. Moving from hypnotic and stripped-back
techno
into
house
,
jungle
and raw machine-driven rhythms.
For nearly ten years,
Callshop Radio
has been broadcasting live to the world. What began in a backyard in Düsseldorf as part of the interdisciplinary cultural space ROOOOOM has, over the years, evolved into an established community radio, building studios in Leipzig, Paris, and Vienna. The
diversity of genres
reflects the many facets of the scenes that Callshop Radio calls home.

Alongside its ongoing radio program, events have always been a core part of the collective. Collaborations with festivals, clubs, museums, and concert venues create opportunities to bring the station’s hosts onto the stage beyond the studio. The focus remains on connecting artists from different locations and shaping events collaboratively.

On June 13, the radio stays true to this vision: for the Annex Floor at Open Ground, alongside one guest and two residents from the Düsseldorf and Leipzig locations have been invited.

Admission 25 €
description.old:
For nearly ten years, Callshop Radio has been broadcasting live to the world. What began in a backyard in Düsseldorf as part of the interdisciplinary cultural space ROOOOOM has, over the years, evolved into an established community radio, building studios in Leipzig, Paris, and Vienna. The diversity of genres reflects the many facets of the scenes that Callshop Radio calls home.

Alongside its ongoing radio program, events have always been a core part of the collective. Collaborations with festivals, clubs, museums, and concert venues create opportunities to bring the station’s hosts onto the stage beyond the studio. The focus remains on connecting artists from different locations and shaping events collaboratively.

On June 13, the radio stays true to this vision: for the Annex Floor at Open Ground, alongside one guest and two residents from the Düsseldorf and Leipzig locations have been invited.

Admission 25 €

description.new:
Freifeld begins with a
Dub-Set
by Tasha and Stojche, before moving between
deep house
,
techno
and
bass-informed
club music. Sensual vocal-led house, raw vinyl-driven techno rooted in Detroit, and
UK bass
,
breaks
and
jungle
unfold in fluid sets shaped by a music-first attitude and an unpolished underground energy.
This night, Annex will be rooted in
DIY soundsystem culture
and
deep bass
. Moving between dub, steppers, broken UK rhythms, experimental electronics and 4x4 basslines, shaped by a collective approach to selection and a strong focus on low frequencies and community energy.

Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

Experimental
electronics
,
dub
,
techno
and sound-based performance. Shaped by a focus on sound as material, spatial thinking and deep listening, the night unfolds between club energy and more abstract, research-driven sonic forms.
A night moving between
house
,
techno
,
electro
and
breaks
, connecting scenes from the Ruhr area, Berlin, Cologne and beyond. From driving, groovy techno and acid- and synth-driven sounds to hypnotic, break- and house-influenced sets, the evening unfolds as a continuous flow between club energy, live elements and experimental sound forms.
Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

Born out of community driven events in studios and shaped by a strong sense of scene identity, a small group of like minded individuals formed around recaved.collective, united by a shared passion for electronic music. While remaining open to other genres, Recaved dedicates this night entirely to the more serious facets of the sound. No trends, just pure techno.

Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

Mzansi Bass at Open Ground
celebrates the launch of the forthcoming compilation of the same name, curated by Shannen SP, to be released with Colombian label Tratratrax on the 12th of June.

Built with
100% new material
, Mzansi Bass centers artists pushing the boundaries of
bass music in South Africa
, spotlighting the continuum of
radical invention
coming out of the region’s electronic sphere. Exploring the
hypnotic, darker, bassier sounds
in between
3-step, Gqom and experimental Amapiano
to form a succinct snapshot of the country’s unique
soundsystem culture
. Deeply rooted in
Black African rave music
from the local scenes of Durban, Johannesburg, Cape Town, and Pretoria, Shannen describes it as
“electronic music that is embodied, trance inducing and deeply physical.”

With a powerful line up featuring artists on the compilation, this party presents a unique offering of
bass music
. Durban’s gqom icon DJ Lag, who has collaborated with artists ranging from Moses Boyd to Novelist to Ape Drums and has taken his intoxicating brand of
Gqom
to clubs all over the world, from Seoul, to Colombia to New York. Shannen SP, whose sets platform the
vanguard of the global club underground
with recent tours in India and East Asia, and support slots for Arca, Batu and Jamie XX. State OFFF, the Zimbabwe-born and Amsterdam-based artist who has been igniting dancefloors worldwide with his bold productions and high-energy sets, blending
Gqom, Baile Funk, Dubstep, and other global sounds
, earning him a spot on Mixmag’s „Top 25 Artists to Watch in 2025.“

Expect to hear
boundary pushing club music
,
hybrid genres
and
lots of unreleased material
.

Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €
description.old:
Mzansi Bass, curated by Shannen SP explores the hypnotic, darker, bassier corners of club music coming out of South Africa and the African continent, covering genres like Gqom, 3 Step, Bacardi - to hybrid sounds that defy categorisation. With a powerful line up featuring Durban’s gqom icon DJ Lag, who has collaborated with artists ranging from Moses Boyd to Novelist to Beyonce and has taken his trance inducing brand of Gqom to clubs all over the world, from Seoul, to Colombia to New York, this party presents a unique offering of bass centred club music. Shannen SP is a creative hybrid who platforms the global club underground. As a DJ, she has supported Arca, Batu, Jamie XX (and warmed up for Bjork), with her commitment to platforming non-western electronic music spanning her A&R work with Hyperdub, NTS and Spitfire Audio. Zimbabwe-born and Amsterdam-based artist State OFFF has been igniting dancefloors worldwide with his bold productions and high-energy sets, blending Gqom, Baile Funk, Dubstep, and other global sounds, earning him a spot on Mixmag’s "Top 25 Artists to Watch in 2025." Expect to hear cutting edge club music, hybrid genres and lots of unreleased material.
Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

description.new:
Bring Your Ass
has been a party series inviting ambassadors of
house music
, guests as Aaron-Carl, Whodat, Marcellus Pittman, and Kyle Hall when he was just 18 years old, way back when internet was not taking that much space. Open Ground is giving the spirit of those parties a new home to continue a deep house journey on a soundsystem comparable in it's intensity to the high technical standard originaly set on US American deep house dancefloors like
The Shelter
.

Let's transform.
description.old: Bring Your Ass has been a party series inviting ambassadors of house music, guests as Aaron-Carl, Whodat, Marcellus Pittman, and Kyle Hall when he was just 18 years old, way back when internet was not taking that much space. Open Ground is giving the spirit of those parties a new home to continue a deep house journey on a soundsystem comparable in it's intensity to the high technical standard originaly set on US American deep house dancefloors like The Shelter. Let's transform.

, earning him a spot on Mixmag’s „Top 25 Artists to Watch in 2025.“

,
hybrid genres
and
lots of unreleased material
.

Echidna
strives to carve out spaces for musical expressions which challenge familiar forms,
motivated by an unwavering desire to bring attention to the unnoticed
- through deep bass, sharp rhythms, and music that refuses to quiet down. Echidna is not designed to comfort: we provoke collision to compose an ode to the unknown. Returning to Annex the third time, Echidna teamed up with Andras_2020’s event series Subtle Wreck.

Subtle Wreck grows from a desire
to deepen the presence of diasporic electronic music
in European contexts, moving beyond the obvious and making space for more layered, experimental, and unsettled approaches to sound.

Together,
Echidna x Subtle Wreck
channel their shared ambitions to curate a night that invites you to indulge in
lurching amplitudes
and
unexpected danceability
. Through live performances and DJ sets, the invited artists twist the frameworks of
post-club
and
leftfield bass
into a
kaleidoscope of abrasive sound
that refuses to quiet down.

Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

floor_subtitle_changes.new: Cotch International presents at Open Ground

description.new:
Born out of community driven events in studios and shaped by a strong sense of scene identity, a small group of like minded individuals formed around recaved.collective, united by a shared passion for electronic music. While remaining open to other genres, Recaved dedicates this night entirely to the more serious facets of the sound. No trends, just pure
techno
.

PRUDE is a Wuppertal-based DJ and producer collective. Formed by DJ Frank, Maruhni and paaradoxx, PRUDE connects club culture with community work through events and workshops.

This time they are back at Annex, bringing Brussel based LAURAVIOLI with them for a night full of energy driven curve balls.

Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

description.new:
The night begins with house-oriented and electro-leaning selections before gradually moving towards bass-heavy, faster and heavier sounds.

Delirium is a collective based in Essen, with members from across the Ruhr region and Düsseldorf. Over the years, we have hosted club nights, collaborated with like-minded collectives and cultural institutions, and contributed to a network of artists and organisers across NRW.

Playing at Open Ground offers a chance to focus entirely on the musical side of Delirium. Made up of people with different musical backgrounds and interests, Delirium has never been tied to a single sound. That diversity is reflected in this lineup.

Admission 20 € / before 23:00 15 €

WAXWORX ist ein Projekt von LØBER und KLEYN aus Koblenz, entstanden aus der gemeinsamen Leidenschaft für Vinylkultur und die Kunst des DJings.

Ihr Sound verbindet Generationen. Roher, hypnotischer Techno mit Wurzeln in den 2020ern, geprägt von der Energie, den Texturen und der Ästhetik der 90er und frühen 2000er.

Im Fokus stehen kompromisslose Selektion, präzises Mixing und lange, treibende Übergänge.

Sorgfältig ausgewählte Schallplatten, musikalisches Storytelling über lange Spannungsbögen und ein kompromissloser Fokus auf den Dancefloor.

Erwartet tiefe Grooves, rohe Energie und eine Selektion, die für massive Soundsysteme gemacht ist.
